21.correct: In the absence of the Vice-President, the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha presides over its sittings.

wrong.A: The Lok Sabha Speaker presides over the Lok Sabha, not the Rajya Sabha.
wrong.B: The President does not preside over the Rajya Sabha.
wrong.D: The Prime Minister does not preside over either House.

21.fact: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

22.correct: In the Giddha dance, 'boliyan' are the traditional rhyming verses/songs sung during the performance.

wrong.A: They are not types of costumes.
wrong.C: They are not specific dance steps.

22.fact: Boliyan often express folk wit, social commentary and emotions.

23.correct: Lonar Lake in Maharashtra is of volcanic/meteoritic origin - it formed in a crater created by a meteorite impact in basalt.

wrong.A: Sambhar is a saline inland lake, not volcanic.
wrong.B: Loktak is a freshwater lake with floating phumdis.

23.fact: Lonar is one of the few saline soda lakes formed by a meteorite impact.

24.correct: V.V. Giri was the only person elected President of India as an independent candidate (1969).

wrong.A: Zail Singh was a Congress nominee.
wrong.B: Neelam Sanjiva Reddy was a party-backed candidate.
wrong.D: Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed was a Congress nominee.

24.fact: V.V. Giri won the 1969 election after a 'conscience vote' split the Congress.

25.correct: The Tomb of Itmad-ud-Daula in Agra is often called the 'Baby Taj'; it marked the shift towards white-marble Mughal architecture.

wrong.B: Bibi Ka Maqbara (Aurangabad) is called the 'Mini Taj', a later imitation.
wrong.C: The Moti Masjid is a mosque, not this tomb.
wrong.D: Humayun's Tomb is an earlier red-sandstone structure.

25.fact: Itmad-ud-Daula's tomb was built by Nur Jahan for her father; it pioneered pietra dura inlay.

26.correct: The standard kabaddi court for women measures 12 m x 8 m.

wrong.A: 10 m x 8 m is not the women's court size.
wrong.D: 13 m x 11 m is the men's court size.

26.fact: The men's kabaddi court is larger, at 13 m x 10 m.

27.correct: A key function of NITI Aayog is to act as a policy think-tank and to promote cooperative federalism between the Centre and the states.

wrong.A: It does not allocate plan funds (that role ended with the Planning Commission).
wrong.C: Monetary policy is controlled by the RBI.
wrong.D: Elections are conducted by the Election Commission.

27.fact: NITI Aayog (2015) replaced the Planning Commission and has no fund-allocation powers.

28.correct: In Chola-era agriculture, the canals from the Kaveri river provided fertile silt and moisture to the fields, supporting prosperous farming.

wrong.B: They were for irrigation, not military protection.
wrong.C: They were primarily for irrigation, not goods transport.
wrong.D: Their main role was irrigation, not fishing/boat transport.

28.fact: The fertile Kaveri delta was the heartland of the Chola economy.

29.correct: IAS officer Ashok Khemka, known for his many transfers during his career, retired in April 2025.

wrong.A: Sujata Saunik is a serving senior officer, not the one described.
wrong.C: Rajiv Topno is not the officer cited.
wrong.D: Durga Shakti Nagpal is not the officer who retired here.

29.fact: Ashok Khemka was transferred over 50 times during his three-decade career.

30.correct: As of May 2025, Heeralal Samariya was the Chief Information Commissioner (CIC) of India.

wrong.A: Y.K. Sinha was a previous CIC.
wrong.C: Sudhir Bhargava was an earlier CIC.
wrong.D: Suresh Chandra is not the CIC.

30.fact: The CIC heads the Central Information Commission under the RTI Act, 2005.

31.correct: Under Section 290 of the BNSS, 2023, an application for plea bargaining can be filed within 30 days from the date of framing of charges.
31.fact: The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) replaced the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC).

32.correct: In Part V of the Constitution, Chapter I ('The Executive') deals with the Union executive - the President, Vice-President and Council of Ministers.

wrong.C: Chapter III deals with legislative powers of the President.
wrong.D: Chapter IV deals with the Union Judiciary.

32.fact: Part V covers the Union, and Part VI covers the States.

33.correct: Vultures are often called nature's scavengers (sanitation workers) because they feed on dead animal carcasses, helping keep the environment clean.

wrong.C: Lions are predators, not scavengers in this sense.
wrong.D: Tigers are predators, not nature's cleaners.

33.fact: India's vulture populations crashed due to the veterinary drug diclofenac, now banned for cattle.

34.correct: The Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR) evolved into ISRO in 1969.

wrong.A: 1962 is when INCOSPAR was set up, not ISRO.
wrong.C: 1972 is when the Department of Space was created.

34.fact: Vikram Sarabhai is regarded as the father of the Indian space programme.

35.correct: The new light-coloured turmeric variety developed by the Indian Institute of Spices Research (IISR), Kozhikode, is named 'IISR Surya'.

wrong.A: 'IISR Jyoti' is not the new turmeric variety named.

35.fact: The IISR develops improved varieties of spices like turmeric, pepper and cardamom.

36.correct: Sachin Tendulkar scored his 100th international century at Dhaka (Mirpur), against Bangladesh, in 2012.

wrong.B: It was not scored in Mumbai.

36.fact: It was Sachin's only century against Bangladesh and his historic 100th ton.

37.correct: The World Bank Land Conference 2025 was held in Washington D.C.

wrong.A: It was not held in New York.
wrong.B: It was not held in Brasilia.
wrong.D: It was not held in Shanghai.

37.fact: The World Bank is headquartered in Washington D.C.

38.correct: 'Chakri' is a traditional music/folk form associated with Jammu and Kashmir.

wrong.C: It is not from Madhya Pradesh.

38.fact: Chakri is a popular folk-music style of the Jammu region, often played on the harmonium and rabab.

39.correct: The Network Readiness Index 2024 evaluated economies on 4 main pillars (Technology, People, Governance and Impact).

wrong.B: 6 is not the number of pillars.

39.fact: The Network Readiness Index assesses how well countries leverage information and communication technologies.

40.correct: The study 'Report on Child Rights in India: An Overview of the Past Decade' was carried out as a desk review by Child Rights and You (CRY).

wrong.B: Oxfam India did not conduct this particular study.
wrong.C: Pratham is known for the ASER education report, not this study.
wrong.D: SaveLIFE Foundation works on road safety.

40.fact: CRY (Child Rights and You) is a well-known NGO working for the rights of underprivileged children.
